page.primary_keyword: Marbella yacht charter July peak season
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter in July: Peak Season Tips & Availability

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella yacht charter July peak season with insider tips, boat availability, and pricing. Book early for Puerto Banús departures from €749/2h.
data.summary: A practical guide to booking a Marbella yacht charter during July peak season, covering availability, pricing, top tips for securing your preferred boat, and local highlights along the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: July is peak season; book 4–6 weeks ahead for best availability.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Prices start at €749/2h (Tier A: Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) including skipper, drinks, and VAT.
Mangusta 80 variants (24m, 12 pax) cost €4,719 minimum 4h with a free jet ski.
Other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94, Pershing 46) are priced on request via WhatsApp.
Every charter includes fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
July sea temperatures average 24°C; calm mornings are ideal for cruising.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book a Marbella yacht charter for July?
faq.a: For July peak season, book at least 4–6 weeks ahead. Our most popular boats — the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and Mangusta 80 — often sell out by mid-June. Last-minute availability is rare, especially for weekends and midday slots.

faq.q: What is the cheapest yacht charter option in July?
faq.a: The most affordable option is our Tier A boats: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include a licensed skipper, drinks, snacks, fuel, and VAT.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There is no corkage fee.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a large group in July?
faq.a: For groups up to 12 guests, consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719 for 4h with a free jet ski), the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). The latter two are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What are the best destinations to visit on a July yacht charter from Marbella?
faq.a: Popular July routes include cruising to Cala del Faro for swimming, anchoring off Cabopino beach, or heading east to Cala Cortés. For a longer trip, sail to Estepona or Sotogrande. July seas are calm in the morning, making it ideal for exploring.

faq.q: Is the Mangusta 80 jet ski included for the full charter duration?
faq.a: The free jet ski is included with the Mangusta 80 (white or grey variants) for the entire charter duration, starting from the 4-hour minimum booking. Usage is subject to safety briefing and the skipper's discretion.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for July charters?
faq.a: We recommend checking with our team at booking for the exact terms. Generally, cancellations made more than 14 days in advance may receive a full refund, while later cancellations may incur a partial charge. We advise travel insurance for peak season.

faq.q: How do I book a boat that is 'pricing on request'?
faq.a: Simply contact us via WhatsApp (the number is on our website) with your preferred boat, date, and number of guests. We will respond within a few hours with a custom quote and availability. Boats like the Ferretti 94, Pershing 46, and Maiora 26m are priced this way.

data.body_html:
July is the heart of summer on the Costa del Sol, and a Marbella yacht charter July peak season booking is one of the most sought-after experiences along the Mediterranean. With sea temperatures hitting 24°C, long sunny days, and vibrant marinas, demand for boats departing Puerto Banús skyrockets. This guide covers everything you need to secure your ideal charter — from pricing and availability to insider tips for navigating the busiest month of the year.

Why July is Peak Season for Marbella Yacht Charters

July brings the highest concentration of visitors to Marbella, drawn by the Golden Mile’s glamour, Puerto Banús nightlife, and near-guaranteed sunshine. The combination of school holidays, European summer breaks, and perfect sailing conditions means that Marbella yacht charter July peak season inventory is limited. Boats like the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 often get booked weeks in advance. If you want to explore the coastline from Cabopino to Estepona, early planning is non-negotiable.

Our Fleet: Availability and Pricing in July

We operate a diverse fleet from Puerto Banús. Below is a snapshot of what is typically available in July, along with pricing. Note that all charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish IVA.

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (July)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h – €2,299/8h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h – €2,299/8h
Mangusta 80 (any variant)24m12€4,719 min 4h (jet ski included)
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12Pricing on request (WhatsApp)
Ferretti 9429m12Pricing on request (WhatsApp)
Pershing 4614m10Pricing on request (WhatsApp)
Maiora 26m26m12Pricing on request (WhatsApp)

For the full fleet list, visit our Marbella yacht charter homepage.

Top Tips for Booking a July Charter

  • Book early: Aim for 4–6 weeks in advance. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are often fully booked by mid-June.
  • Choose morning departures: July afternoons can be hot (32°C+). A 10:00 or 11:00 departure lets you enjoy calmer seas and cooler temperatures.
  • Consider a full-day charter: An 8-hour booking (€2,299 for Tier A) allows you to cruise to Cala del Faro, anchor for lunch, and return via Cabopino without rushing.
  • Ask about jet skis: The Mangusta 80 includes a free jet ski — a huge hit for groups wanting water sports.
  • Flexibility pays: If your first-choice boat is unavailable, ask about alternatives like the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) — both priced on request.

Best July Itineraries from Puerto Banús

July’s calm mornings and clear waters make it ideal for coastal exploration. Here are three recommended routes:

  • East to Cala Cortés: A 30-minute cruise from Puerto Banús brings you to this secluded cove near Marbella Marina. Swim in turquoise water, then head back for lunch at a chiringuito.
  • West to Estepona: Sail 12 NM to Estepona’s marina, passing the dunes of Cabopino. Anchor for a swim stop at Playa del Cristo before returning.
  • Full-day to Sotogrande: For a longer trip (8 hours), head 25 NM to Sotogrande. Enjoy lunch at a beach club and spot the La Concha mountain backdrop on the return.

What to Expect: Weather, Crowds, and Sea Conditions

July in Marbella averages 30°C air temperature, 24°C sea temperature, and 10–11 hours of sunshine. The levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, so morning charters are smoother. Puerto Banús is busiest on weekends and during the Marbella International Film Festival (usually late June/early July). Midweek charters offer more space and quieter anchorages. For real-time updates, check our blog for local conditions.

How to Secure a Boat Priced on Request

Boats like the Ferretti 94, Pershing 46, and Maiora 26m do not have fixed hourly rates. To book one, simply send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date, number of guests, and desired duration. We will reply within a few hours with a custom quote. Availability for these luxury yachts is especially tight in July, so contact us as early as possible. Browse our full fleet on the Marbella yacht charter page.

Frequently asked questions

How far in advance should I book a Marbella yacht charter for July?

For July peak season, book at least 4–6 weeks ahead. Our most popular boats — the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and Mangusta 80 — often sell out by mid-June. Last-minute availability is rare, especially for weekends and midday slots.

What is the cheapest yacht charter option in July?

The most affordable option is our Tier A boats: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include a licensed skipper, drinks, snacks, fuel, and VAT.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There is no corkage fee.

Which boats are best for a large group in July?

For groups up to 12 guests, consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719 for 4h with a free jet ski), the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). The latter two are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What are the best destinations to visit on a July yacht charter from Marbella?

Popular July routes include cruising to Cala del Faro for swimming, anchoring off Cabopino beach, or heading east to Cala Cortés. For a longer trip, sail to Estepona or Sotogrande. July seas are calm in the morning, making it ideal for exploring.

Is the Mangusta 80 jet ski included for the full charter duration?

The free jet ski is included with the Mangusta 80 (white or grey variants) for the entire charter duration, starting from the 4-hour minimum booking. Usage is subject to safety briefing and the skipper's discretion.

What is the cancellation policy for July charters?

We recommend checking with our team at booking for the exact terms. Generally, cancellations made more than 14 days in advance may receive a full refund, while later cancellations may incur a partial charge. We advise travel insurance for peak season.

How do I book a boat that is 'pricing on request'?

Simply contact us via WhatsApp (the number is on our website) with your preferred boat, date, and number of guests. We will respond within a few hours with a custom quote and availability. Boats like the Ferretti 94, Pershing 46, and Maiora 26m are priced this way.
